name: Pine Nut Crisps
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine granulated sugar, flour and beaten egg whites in a large bowl. Reserve the egg yolks for another use.
2. Stir to blend the dry ingredients with the egg whites until well combined.
3. Add vanilla, orange extract, and melted butter to the mixture. Fold gently until incorporated.
4. Mix in the pine nuts evenly.
5. Drop spoonfuls of the batter onto lightly greased baking sheets.
6. Arrange the cookies so that 3 or 4 fit on a single layer of the large baking sheet.
7. Bake at 350°F for 8-10 minutes, or until the edges are browned and crisp.
8. Remove from the oven and let cool on a wire rack.
This recipe yields approximately 2 dozen cookies. Each cookie contains about: 79 calories; 23 milligrams sodium; 3 milligrams cholesterol; 4 grams fat; 11 grams carbohydrates; 2 grams protein; and 0.05 grams fiber.
